heading: Review of participation in WTO
label: 3535 Review of participation in WTO
notes: References in TextSections 101(a) and 2(9) of the Uruguay Round Agreements Act, referred to in subsec. (c)(1), are classified to sections 3511(a) and 3501(9), respectively, of this title.

Effective DateSection effective on the date on which the WTO Agreement enters into force with respect to the United States (), see , set out as a note under .Jan. 1, 1995section 130 of Pub. L. 103–465section 3531 of this title

Uruguay Round Agreements: Entry Into ForceThe Uruguay Round Agreements, including the World Trade Organization Agreement and agreements annexed to that Agreement, as referred to in , entered into force with respect to the United States on . See note set out under .section 3511(d) of this titlesection 3511 of this titleJan. 1, 1995

children.heading: Report on operation of WTO
children.label: a Report on operation of WTO

children.text: after the end of the 5-year period beginning on the date on which the WTO Agreement enters into force with respect to the United States, and
after the end of every 5-year period thereafter,

children.heading: Congressional disapproval of U.S. participation in WTO
children.label: b Congressional disapproval of U.S. participation in WTO

children.heading: General rule
children.label: 1 General rule
children.text: The approval of the Congress, provided under , of the WTO Agreement shall cease to be effective if, and only if, a joint resolution described in subsection (c) is enacted into law pursuant to the provisions of paragraph (2).section 3511(a) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: the Congress adopts and transmits the joint resolution to the President before the end of the 90-day period (excluding any day described in ), beginning on the date on which the Congress receives a report referred to in subsection (a), andsection 2194(b) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: if the President vetoes the joint resolution, each House of Congress votes to override that veto on or before the later of the last day of the 90-day period referred to in clause (i) or the last day of the 15-day period (excluding any day described in ) beginning on the date on which the Congress receives the veto message from the President.section 2194(b) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: A joint resolution to which this section applies may be introduced at any time on or after the date on which the President transmits to the Congress a report described in subsection (a), and before the end of the 90-day period referred to in subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Joint resolutions
children.label: 1 Joint resolutions
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “joint resolution” means only a joint resolution of the 2 Houses of Congress, the matter after the resolving clause of which is as follows: “That the Congress withdraws its approval, provided under section 101(a) of the Uruguay Round Agreements Act, of the WTO Agreement as defined in section 2(9) of that Act.”

children.label: A
children.text: Joint resolutions may be introduced in either House of the Congress by any member of such House.

children.label: B
children.text: Subject to the provisions of this subsection, the provisions of subsections (b), (d), (e), and (f) of apply to joint resolutions to the same extent as such provisions apply to resolutions under such section.section 2192 of this title

children.label: C
children.text: If the committee of either House to which a joint resolution has been referred has not reported it by the close of the 45th day after its introduction (excluding any day described in ), such committee shall be automatically discharged from further consideration of the joint resolution and it shall be placed on the appropriate calendar.section 2194(b) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: the Senate to consider any joint resolution unless it has been reported by the Committee on Finance or the committee has been discharged under subparagraph (C); or

children.label: ii
children.text: the House of Representatives to consider any joint resolution unless it has been reported by the Committee on Ways and Means or the committee has been discharged under subparagraph (C).

children.label: E
children.text: A motion in the House of Representatives to proceed to the consideration of a joint resolution may only be made on the second legislative day after the calendar day on which the Member making the motion announces to the House his or her intention to do so.

children.heading: Consideration of second resolution not in order
children.label: 3 Consideration of second resolution not in order
children.text: It shall not be in order in either the House of Representatives or the Senate to consider a joint resolution (other than a joint resolution received from the other House), if that House has previously adopted a joint resolution under this section.

children.heading: Rules of House of Representatives and Senate
children.label: d Rules of House of Representatives and Senate

children.text: as an exercise of the rulemaking power of the House of Representatives and the Senate, respectively, and as such is deemed a part of the rules of each House, respectively, and such procedures supersede other rules only to the extent that they are inconsistent with such other rules; and

with the full recognition of the constitutional right of either House to change the rules (so far as relating to the procedures of that House) at any time, in the same manner, and to the same extent as any other rule of that House.
